customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high engagement. Customer is a first-time homeowner, self-initiated the inquiry, and is doing deliberate comparison shopping — not impulsive, not skeptical of solar generally. Primary concern is making sure they're getting the best deal, not whether solar works.
unresolved_concerns: Sunrun quote of ~$160/month with two batteries is the unchallenged benchmark — customer has not been given a reason to expect Ion's offer to be different or better.
Customer asked about plan terms (15-year vs 20-year vs 25-year) and received uncertain hedging — this question will come up again at the appointment.

Bill photo not yet received as of end of call. If this is a paid lead, the sit cannot be confirmed until the bill is captured. Lead type unknown from transcript — setter should confirm bill receipt before the appointment date.
sensitive_topics_raised: Customer is a first-time homeowner (six months in, new construction) — this is their first solar experience; no prior install history.
Customer is on ComEd hourly pricing, which affects how savings are calculated — closer should be aware of the pricing structure.
Customer has an EV and specifically requested two batteries for multi-day outage coverage — battery sizing is a priority for this customer, not an add-on.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for Friday, May 8 at 3PM.
Text with appointment details to be sent after the call.
Specialist will go into battery details during the appointment.
Specialist will cover plan term options during the appointment.
closer_should_know: This customer has a concrete Sunrun quote (~$160/month, two batteries) and is doing real comparison work — they will evaluate Ion's proposal directly against that number. Lead with the design and let the numbers speak; do not assume the customer is sold on Ion. The EV load (1,100–1,400 kWh/month) and the two-battery preference are the two strongest anchors for the proposal. The rep also introduced uncertainty about Ion's battery lineup (implied a second brand beyond Powerwall) — correct this cleanly at the start of the appointment before it becomes a trust issue.
